payload.title: METROPOLITAN TRANSPORTATION AUTHORITY July Financial Plan - 2022 Mid-Year Forecast Accrual Statement of Operations by Category November 2022 Monthly ($ in millions)

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenue was favorable by $173.3M (5.0%) YTD November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, contributed by higher average fare at NYCT, MNR, LIRR, and MTA Bus, partially offset by unfavorable SIR.
payload.subject: Farebox Revenue

payload.claim_text: Vehicle Toll Revenue was unfavorable by ($7.1M) (3.6%) for November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, due to traffic volume below forecasted levels.
payload.subject: Vehicle Toll Revenue

payload.claim_text: Payroll (Non-Reimbursable) was favorable by $29.9M (6.0%) for November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, mainly due to vacancies at NYCT, MTA HQ, LIRR, B&T and MTA Bus.
payload.subject: Payroll

payload.claim_text: Overtime (Non-Reimbursable) was unfavorable by ($19.5M) (24.1%) for November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, due to higher vacancy/absentee coverage requirements at NYCT and MNR.
payload.subject: Overtime

payload.claim_text: Subsidies were unfavorable by ($56.9M) (6.8%) for November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, reflecting unfavorable results for PBT ($76.0M), MR1-2 ($7.6M), State Operating Assistance-18b, City Subsidy for MTA Bus, and Local Operating Assistance, partially offset by favorable PMT ($26.9M) and PHV ($10.8M).
payload.subject: Subsidies

payload.claim_text: Debt Service for November 2022 was $259.6 million, $48.7 million (23.1%) unfavorable vs Mid-Year Forecast due to a cash call of outstanding DTF and TRB Bonds, partially offset by reversal of November 15 prefunding done in October 2022.
payload.subject: Debt Service

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenue was favorable by $22.7M (6.7%) for November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, mainly due to higher ridership and average fare.

Higher average fare contributed to YTD favorable farebox revenue variances of $113.0M at NYCT, $31.4M at MNR, $13.3M at LIRR, and $14.6M at MTA Bus, partially offset by ($1.0M) unfavorable at SIR due to lower ridership.
Overtime (Non-Reimbursable) was unfavorable by ($214.5M) (26.0%) YTD November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, due to higher vacancy/absentee coverage and adverse weather.

Health and Welfare (Non-Reimbursable) was favorable by $36.4M (26.4%) for November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, with NYCT favorable by $28.6M mainly due to fewer claims and timing of prescription drug rebate credits.

Subsidies were favorable by $171.0M (2.5%) YTD November 2022 vs Mid-Year Forecast, reflecting favorable results for PMT ($325.7M) and Urban Tax ($76.5M), partially offset by unfavorable City Subsidy for MTA Bus ($141.9M) and PBT ($61.9M).

Year-to-Date Debt Service expenses were $2,857.50 million, $94.9 million (3.4%) unfavorable due to cash call of outstanding DTF and TRB bonds, partially offset by lower than budgeted variable rates and timing of debt issuance.

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are subject to review and adjustment.
payload.description: November 2022 monthly actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in YTD results.

payload.claim_text: GASB 75 OPEB Expense Adjustment for November 2022 reflects the impact of a GAAP change in OPEB liability. MTA Bus was favorable by $11.4M for the month and $66.4M YTD, partially offset by unfavorable ($1.7M) at B&T YTD.
GASB 68 Pension Expense Adjustment reflects Agencies' adjustments to account for net pension liability. MTA Bus was favorable by $6.5M for November and $39.0M YTD.
Insurance was favorable by $4.9M (87.1%) for November 2022 and $28.8M (79.5%) YTD, due to the timing of policy renewals at FMTAC, NYCT, MTA Bus, and LIRR.
Claims were favorable by $13.8M (35.2%) for November 2022 and $55.3M (14.0%) YTD, mainly due to timing variances at FMTAC and MTA Bus.

Paratransit Service Contracts were unfavorable by ($2.5M) (6.6%) for November 2022 due to higher expenses at NYCT mainly due to timing, but favorable by $12.8M (3.3%) YTD reflecting fewer trips and timing of support costs.
Professional Service Contracts (Non-Reimbursable) were favorable by $19.6M (23.0%) for November 2022, attributable to timing of MTA bond services and real estate expenses at NYCT.
Materials & Supplies (Non-Reimbursable) were favorable by $20.3M (28.6%) for November 2022, with favorable variances at LIRR ($11.6M), NYCT ($3.8M), MTA Bus ($2.8M), and MNR ($2.4M).

Materials & Supplies (Non-Reimbursable) were favorable by $119.7M (19.1%) YTD November 2022, with NYCT ($47.6M), MNR ($33.0M), LIRR ($23.3M), and MTA Bus ($16.6M) reflecting timing of fleet modifications and vehicle materials.
OPEB Current Payment (Non-Reimbursable) was favorable by $31.7M (47.4%) for November 2022, with NYCT favorable by $29.7M mainly due to fewer claims and timing of prescription drug rebate credits.

Pensions (Non-Reimbursable) were favorable by $7.2M (6.4%) for November 2022, primarily due to timing at MTA HQ and LIRR, higher capital reimbursement offsets at B&T, and lower pension expense at NYCT.
Electric Power was favorable by $0.7M (1.3%) for November 2022, largely due to lower consumption at NYCT ($2.1M) and LIRR ($1.0M), partially offset by unfavorable MNR ($2.2M) due to higher rates.

Fuel was favorable by $1.3M (5.3%) for November 2022, with lower consumption at NYCT ($1.0M) and lower fraction rates at MNR ($0.5M), partially offset by unfavorable LIRR ($0.6M) due to higher consumption and prices.

Other Fringe Benefits (Non-Reimbursable) were favorable by $5.8M (6.7%) for November 2022, with LIRR favorable by $4.2M due to lower FELA indemnity reserves and MNR favorable by $2.2M, partially offset by unfavorable MTA Bus ($3.8M) due to higher workers compensation.
